source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown, two Silicon (Si) abrupt p-n junction diodes are fabricated with uniform donor doping concentration of \( N_{D1} = 10^{14} \, \text{cm}^{-3} \) and \( N_{D2} = 10^{16} \, \text{cm}^{-3} \) in the n-regions of the diodes, and uniform acceptor doping concentration of \( N_{A1} = 10^{14} \, \text{cm}^{-3} \) and \( N_{A2} = 10^{16} \, \text{cm}^{-3} \) in the p-regions of the diodes, respectively. Assuming that the reverse bias voltage is much greater than the built-in potentials of the diodes, the ratio \( \frac{C_2}{C_1} \) of their reverse bias capacitances for the same applied reverse bias is ________.
extraction_notes: Removed leading instruction/hint text and kept the question body.
Image field contains a PIL.Image object representation, not a path/URL, so no image path was extracted.

issues: metadata.extraction_notes states that no image path was extracted from the source image field.
metadata.image_paths is empty, while top-level image_paths contains one image path.

image_dependency: The question explicitly says 'As shown', indicating some dependence on the figure.
Top-level fields state requires_image=true and text_dominant=false.
An image asset exists in the registry and registry_passed=true.
However, the textual prompt already includes the key doping values and the target ratio, so the image does not appear to be the sole carrier of the needed numerical information.
multi_step: Solving requires recalling the reverse-bias junction capacitance dependence for an abrupt p-n junction.
The solver must combine the given NA and ND values for two different diodes and compare the resulting capacitances under the same reverse bias.
This is a numeric free-response question rather than recognition from choices, so some derivation/simplification is needed.
verifiability: The expected answer is numeric ('10'), which is easy to compare against a derived result.
The problem is a standard physics/electronics formula application, so the reasoning path is checkable.
registry_passed=true supports basic asset availability, though some image-path metadata issues remain.

risk_flags: Question references a figure, so any geometry/area assumptions encoded only in the image could affect interpretation.
